title: Where the Iran Conflict Goes From Here
gist: An FP Live event landing page previewing a July 20, 2026 interview with ex-NSC Iran director Nate Swanson, whose on-record predictive track record on Iran is highlighted, alongside teaser summaries of other recent FP Live episodes covering trans-Atlantic relations, Ukraine's battlefield momentum, Cuba, and SpaceX/Musk.

insights.claim: Nate Swanson, a former State Department official who served as director for Iran at the National Security Council before being forced out of his role by the Trump administration last year, is cited as having accurately predicted how Iran would respond to U.S. strikes and how Tehran might overplay its hand after signing its memorandum of understanding.
insights.speaker: Dana Sherne
insights.so_what: Swanson's demonstrated forecasting record on Iran, made from outside government, positions him as a source worth tracking for near-term Iran-policy calls.
insights.locator: One advisor the White House could have turned to

insights.claim: At a July 14, 2026 FP Live debate, Nathalie Tocci argued the trans-Atlantic relationship cannot be rebuilt as it was because the liberal order that gave it context is gone and a fundamental breach of trust has occurred, while Alexandra de Hoop Scheffer countered that trans-Atlanticism was never based only on shared values and that Europe's surge in defense spending signals its emergence as a geopolitically mature partner.
insights.speaker: Dana Sherne
insights.so_what: Frames two competing theses on transatlantic durability under Trump's second term, useful for calibrating European-defense and NATO-cohesion exposure.
insights.locator: Nathalie Tocci argues it cannot be rebuilt as it was

insights.claim: At the NATO summit in Ankara the week of July 9, 2026, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky argued Ukraine's long-range drones have brought the war home to Russians, a strain that even President Vladimir Putin has acknowledged, and used this as leverage to press NATO allies to cement their support.
insights.speaker: Dana Sherne
insights.so_what: Putin's acknowledgment of domestic strain from Ukrainian drone strikes is itself a signal of the campaign's political bite, relevant to gauging ceasefire prospects.
insights.locator: But this time, he could argue that Ukraine's long-range drones

insights.claim: After the U.S. ousted Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro in January 2026 and took out Iran's senior leadership in February 2026, Trump turned to Cuba, and a five-month U.S. oil blockade has pushed Havana to restructure its economy and publicly welcome diplomacy with Washington.
insights.speaker: Dana Sherne
insights.so_what: Establishes a repeated Trump-administration playbook of economic blockade followed by regime pressure across Venezuela, Iran, and now Cuba, relevant for modeling which country could be next.
insights.locator: After the United States ousted Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro

insights.claim: SpaceX's Nasdaq listing briefly made CEO Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire, and historian Quinn Slobodian, co-author of the book Muskism, argues Musk has designs on controlling terrestrial discourse using technological leverage based in space.
insights.speaker: Dana Sherne
insights.so_what: Frames SpaceX's market cap milestone as a governance question, not just a valuation one, worth watching as space infrastructure concentrates in private hands.
